syft.name: Syft
syft.description: Generate Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) from container images, filesystems, and archives. Outputs CycloneDX, SPDX, and more.

config_fields.label: Binary Path
config_fields.help: Custom path to syft binary (leave blank to use PATH)

config_fields.label: Default Output Format
config_fields.help: Default SBOM output format

config_fields.label: Scan Scope
config_fields.help: squashed = final image layer; all-layers = every layer

config_fields.label: Anchore Enterprise URL
config_fields.help: Upload SBOMs to an Anchore Enterprise instance (optional)

config_fields.label: Anchore Upload Path
config_fields.help: API path for Anchore SBOM uploads

grype.name: Grype
grype.description: Vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems. Uses Anchore's vulnerability database and accepts SBOM input.

config_fields.label: Binary Path
config_fields.help: Custom path to grype binary

config_fields.label: Auto-update DB
config_fields.help: Automatically update the vulnerability database on each run

config_fields.label: Fail on Severity
config_fields.help: Exit non-zero when vulnerabilities at this level (or above) are found

config_fields.label: Only Fixed Vulns
config_fields.help: Only report vulnerabilities that have a fix available

config_fields.label: Generate CPEs
config_fields.help: Generate CPEs for packages that don't have them (improves match rate)

config_fields.label: DB Update URL
config_fields.help: Custom vulnerability DB feed URL (for air-gapped environments)

trivy.name: Trivy
trivy.description: Comprehensive vulnerability scanner for container images, filesystems, git repos, Kubernetes clusters, and cloud resources.

install_methods.label: Linux (apt)
install_methods.cmd: sudo apt-get install -y wget apt-transport-https gnupg lsb-release && wget -qO - https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-repo/deb/public.key | sudo apt-key add - && echo "deb https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-repo/deb $(lsb_release -sc) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/trivy.list && sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y trivy

config_fields.label: Binary Path
config_fields.help: Custom path to trivy binary

config_fields.label: Severity Filter
config_fields.help: Only report vulnerabilities at these severity levels

config_fields.label: Cache Directory
config_fields.help: Directory for Trivy's vulnerability DB cache (leave blank for default)

config_fields.label: Skip DB Update
config_fields.help: Skip database update on each run (use cached DB)

config_fields.label: Offline Scan
config_fields.help: Disable all network access (requires pre-downloaded DB)

config_fields.label: DB Repository
config_fields.help: Custom vulnerability DB OCI repository (air-gapped environments)

config_fields.label: Ignore Unfixed
config_fields.help: Do not report vulnerabilities with no available fix

nuclei.name: Nuclei
nuclei.description: Fast, template-based vulnerability scanner for URLs and APIs. 9000+ community templates covering CVEs, misconfigurations, and exposures.

config_fields.label: Binary Path
config_fields.help: Custom path to nuclei binary

config_fields.label: Custom Templates Dir
config_fields.help: Path to directory with your custom Nuclei templates

config_fields.label: Rate Limit (req/sec)
config_fields.help: Maximum HTTP requests per second (lower for sensitive targets)

config_fields.label: Concurrency
config_fields.help: Number of concurrent template executions

config_fields.label: Request Timeout (sec)
config_fields.help: HTTP request timeout in seconds

config_fields.label: Retries
config_fields.help: Retry failed requests this many times

config_fields.label: GitHub Token
config_fields.help: GitHub PAT for accessing private template repositories

prowler.name: Prowler
prowler.description: Cloud security assessment against CIS, SOC2, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and NIST for AWS, Azure, and GCP.

config_fields.label: AWS Profile
config_fields.help: AWS CLI profile name from ~/.aws/credentials

config_fields.label: AWS Region
config_fields.help: Default AWS region to scan

config_fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
config_fields.help: AWS access key ID (prefer named profiles over static keys)

config_fields.label: AWS Secret Access Key
config_fields.help: AWS secret access key

config_fields.label: AWS Session Token
config_fields.help: AWS STS session token (for temporary credentials)

config_fields.label: Azure Subscription ID
config_fields.help: Azure subscription to scan

config_fields.label: Azure Tenant ID
config_fields.help: Azure Active Directory tenant ID

config_fields.label: Azure Client ID
config_fields.help: Azure service principal app ID

config_fields.label: Azure Client Secret
config_fields.help: Azure service principal secret

config_fields.label: GCP Project ID
config_fields.help: Google Cloud project to scan

config_fields.label: GCP Service Account JSON
config_fields.help: Path to GCP service account key JSON file

scoutsuite.name: ScoutSuite
scoutsuite.description: Multi-cloud security auditing tool for AWS, Azure, GCP, Alibaba, and Oracle. Produces detailed HTML reports.

config_fields.label: AWS Profile
config_fields.help: AWS CLI profile name

config_fields.label: AWS Region
config_fields.help: Default AWS region

config_fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
config_fields.help: AWS access key ID

config_fields.label: AWS Secret Access Key
config_fields.help: AWS secret access key

config_fields.label: Azure Subscription ID
config_fields.help: Azure subscription to audit

config_fields.label: Azure Tenant ID
config_fields.help: Azure AD tenant ID

config_fields.label: GCP Project ID
config_fields.help: Google Cloud project to audit

config_fields.label: GCP Service Account JSON
config_fields.help: Path to GCP service account key JSON file

config_fields.label: Report Output Directory
config_fields.help: Directory to write HTML audit reports

checkov.name: Checkov
checkov.description: Static analysis for IaC — Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes, Dockerfiles, Helm, ARM, and more.

config_fields.label: Bridgecrew API Key
config_fields.help: Enables Bridgecrew cloud policies, reporting, and platform integration

config_fields.label: Skip Checks (comma-separated)
config_fields.help: Checks to skip, e.g. CKV_AWS_18,CKV_AWS_20

config_fields.label: Run Only (comma-separated)
config_fields.help: Run only these specific checks (leave empty to run all)

config_fields.label: Soft Fail Mode
config_fields.help: Always exit 0 — useful for CI pipelines that should not block on findings

config_fields.label: Compact Output
config_fields.help: Suppress passing checks in output

config_fields.label: Quiet Mode
config_fields.help: Only display failing checks

zap.name: OWASP ZAP
zap.description: Industry-leading open-source DAST scanner. Finds XSS, SQL injection, CSRF, missing headers, and hundreds of other vulnerabilities in live web applications and APIs.

config_fields.label: Use Docker (recommended)
config_fields.help: Run ZAP via Docker image. Easiest setup, no native install needed.

config_fields.label: ZAP Daemon Host
config_fields.help: Hostname of a running ZAP daemon (API mode only — ignored in Docker mode)

config_fields.label: ZAP Daemon Port
config_fields.help: Port for the ZAP REST API (API mode only)

config_fields.label: ZAP API Key
config_fields.help: API key set in ZAP Tools > Options > API (API mode only)

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: SBOM generation + vulnerability scanning. Zero cost, zero cloud credentials needed.

scanner.name: Full Scanner
scanner.description: Starter + URL endpoint scanning + IaC misconfiguration checks.

cloud.name: Cloud Security
cloud.description: Full Scanner + multi-cloud posture assessment. Requires cloud provider credentials.

dast.name: DAST Bundle
dast.description: Full Scanner + OWASP ZAP dynamic testing for web applications and APIs.

full.name: Everything
full.description: Complete security suite — all tools. Requires cloud credentials and Docker or native ZAP.

custom.name: Custom
custom.description: Pick exactly the tools you need.
